meta.name: council-golden-test-cases
meta.description: 20 golden test cases for Council v2 verdict engine. Each has input + expected verdict + quality criteria. Use these to evaluate every prompt change.

scoring.reasons_quality: Are reasons specific, evidence-based, not generic? (1-10)
scoring.tone_correct: Brutal honest but respectful? No jargon? (1-10)
scoring.confidence_calibrated: Is confidence score appropriate for available data? (1-10)
scoring.actionable: Can the user DO something with this verdict? (1-10)
scoring.would_share: Would someone screenshot and tweet this? (1-10)

test_cases.input: Instagram clone yapmak istiyorum
test_cases.quality_notes: Should be very confident. Reason: impossible to compete with Instagram. Should NOT be polite about it. Should suggest what to do instead.

test_cases.input: I want to build a tool that converts Figma designs to production React code with 1:1 accuracy
test_cases.quality_notes: Hot market, real pain point, existing traction (Locofy, Anima). Should say GO but flag the competition. Confidence not 95+ because crowded.

test_cases.input: Yapay zeka ile herkesin kendi podcast'ini otomatik oluşturabileceği bir platform
test_cases.quality_notes: Market exists but 'everyone' is wrong target. Should suggest niching down (e.g., businesses, course creators). Generic 'AI podcast' = no differentiation.

test_cases.input: bir uygulama yapmak istiyorum
test_cases.quality_notes: Input is too vague. Confidence should be VERY LOW. Should ask for more detail or say 'I can't evaluate this without more info'. Should NOT hallucinate a specific app idea.

test_cases.input: Online pharmacy where people can order prescription drugs and get them delivered same-day
test_cases.quality_notes: Legal/regulatory is the #1 issue. Should flag FDA, pharmacy license, state-by-state regulation. Should suggest OTC-only or telehealth pivot. Financial model works if legal clears.

test_cases.input: Bir proje yonetim araci yapmak istiyorum, Jira'dan daha basit olacak
test_cases.quality_notes: Extremely saturated: Linear, Notion, Asana, Monday, ClickUp, Height, Plane... 'Simpler Jira' has been tried 100 times. Should name specific competitors. Should suggest extreme niche if anything.

test_cases.input: AI tool that reads legal contracts and highlights unfair clauses for freelancers
test_cases.quality_notes: Specific target (freelancers), real pain point, AI-native, defensible if trained on contract data. Should flag: not legal advice disclaimer needed.

test_cases.input: Smart dog collar that monitors health and sends alerts to vet
test_cases.quality_notes: Good idea but hardware + vet integration = very complex + expensive. Should suggest software-only pivot (existing collar data analysis) or partnership model.

test_cases.input: Platform for companies to manage their AI agent workforce — hiring, monitoring, billing AI agents
test_cases.quality_notes: Timing is perfect (2026 AI agent explosion). Early mover advantage. Should flag: market still forming, might be too early. But the trend is clear.

test_cases.input: Turkiye'de restoranlara ozel bir online siparis sistemi, Yemeksepeti'ne alternatif
test_cases.quality_notes: Yemeksepeti/Getir/Trendyol Yemek dominate. Capital-intensive, logistics nightmare. Should be very confident DONT. Local market knowledge important.

test_cases.input: SaaS tool that automatically generates SOC2 compliance documentation from your codebase
test_cases.quality_notes: High pain, high willingness to pay, technical moat. Competitors exist (Vanta, Drata) but they're expensive. Should say GO with 'find your wedge' advice.

test_cases.input: A social network for book lovers where you can share highlights and discuss chapters
test_cases.quality_notes: Goodreads exists (Amazon-owned). StoryGraph growing. Problem: social networks need critical mass. Should suggest: NOT a social network, but a tool (highlight manager) that has social features.

test_cases.input: Decentralized freelance marketplace on blockchain
test_cases.quality_notes: Blockchain adds complexity without solving real freelancer problems. Upwork/Fiverr work. Users don't care about decentralization. Payment processing is already solved.

test_cases.input: AI tutor that adapts to each student's learning style and pace, for K-12 math
test_cases.quality_notes: Real problem, parents will pay, AI makes it possible now. But: school sales cycles are brutal. Should say GO for B2C (parents), flag B2B (schools) as hard.

test_cases.input: A platform for urban farmers to sell produce directly to restaurants in Lagos, Nigeria
test_cases.quality_notes: MUST have low confidence — limited data on Lagos market. Should explicitly say 'I don't have enough data on this specific market'. Should NOT hallucinate Lagos-specific statistics.
red_flags_if_output_contains: the Lagos market is worth $X billion

test_cases.input: I want to build Notion but better
test_cases.quality_notes: Notion has $10B+ valuation, massive moat. 'But better' is not a strategy. Should be blunt. Should suggest: what SPECIFIC thing about Notion is broken for WHICH specific user?

test_cases.input: Newsletter platform specifically for indie game developers to build audience
test_cases.quality_notes: Niche is good (indie game devs). But newsletter platforms are commoditized (Substack, Beehiiv, ConvertKit). Should say GO only if there's a game-specific angle (Steam integration, gameplay GIFs in emails).

test_cases.input: Protein folding prediction tool for small biotech companies that can't afford AlphaFold infrastructure
test_cases.quality_notes: Real technical problem. AlphaFold is open but infra is expensive. But: tiny market, very technical, long sales cycle. Confidence medium because domain expertise needed. Should NOT pretend to understand biotech deeply.

test_cases.input: Ikinci el cocuk kiyafeti alis-veris platformu, anneler arasinda
test_cases.quality_notes: Demand exists (parents resell constantly) but marketplace cold start problem. Letgo/Trendyol already used. Should suggest: start as WhatsApp group/community first, not platform. Or Instagram-based.

test_cases.input: ChatGPT wrapper that makes it easier to write marketing copy
test_cases.quality_notes: Classic 'thin wrapper' problem. Jasper, Copy.ai, Writesonic already exist AND struggling. OpenAI/Anthropic keep eating the wrapper layer. Should be very direct: 'You'll be competing with your own API provider.'
red_flags_if_output_contains: AI writing is a growing market

pass_criteria.avg_confidence_calibration: Average >= 7/10, AND no case where confidence is >80 when expected <60
pass_criteria.zero_hallucination: 0 cases where specific numbers/stats are fabricated without source

evaluation_rubric.how_to_run: For each test case: 1) Send input to Council API. 2) Parse JSON output. 3) Check verdict matches expected. 4) Check confidence in expected range. 5) Check output does NOT contain red_flags. 6) Human-rate reasons_quality, tone, confidence_calibration, actionable, would_share (1-10 each).
